blocks.content: Julian was back at his rental by one in the afternoon.
He made coffee. Sat at the study table with the legal pad. Added what Davenport had told him — or rather, what Davenport had chosen not to tell him, which was sometimes more useful.
They'd pulled the call records. Davenport had gone quiet at the mention of them. Quiet the way people go quiet when something is confirmed rather than when something is unknown.

The call records tell him something. Something he's not sharing yet. Which means whoever Marcus was arguing with on Saturday night is either identifiable already — or connected to someone in that house.

blocks.speaker: Mara
blocks.content: Status report. And before you say you're not required to give me one — you're not, but you will anyway because that's how we work.

blocks.speaker: Julian
blocks.content: I gave my formal statement this morning.

blocks.speaker: Julian
blocks.content: The inspector is sharper than he looks. He's holding something back on the phone records.

blocks.speaker: Mara
blocks.content: Julian. You were supposed to be back in the office on Wednesday.

blocks.speaker: Julian
blocks.content: Then reschedule a third time. Mara — someone planned this carefully. I'm not leaving until I understand how.
